---
title: Example training Deep-IMV on a data with a missing view.
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.append('../')

import torch
from torch.optim import AdamW

from diomics.models.fc_imv import FC_Marginal, JointVAE
from diomics.models.multi_mlp import simple_FC, JointMLP

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, confusion_matrix
```

```{python}
lip = pd.read_csv("/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/lip_neg/edata.csv")
metab = pd.read_csv("/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/metab/edata.csv")
pep = pd.read_csv("/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/pep/edata.csv")

lip.set_index("Lipid", inplace = True)
metab.set_index("Metabolite", inplace = True)
pep.set_index("PEPTIDE.EX", inplace = True)

fdata_lip = pd.read_csv('/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/lip_neg/fdata.csv')
fdata_metab = pd.read_csv('/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/metab/fdata.csv')
fdata_pep = pd.read_csv('/Volumes/Data_Harmony/data_to_share_with_UI_developers/ICL102/01_omics_elements/pep/fdata.csv')

# metab has zeros instead of NA :( replace them with NA
metab[metab == 0] = np.nan

# log transform data
lip = np.log2(lip)
metab = np.log2(metab)
pep = np.log2(pep)

# naive imputation
lip.fillna(lip.mean(), inplace = True)
metab.fillna(metab.mean(), inplace = True)
pep.fillna(pep.mean(), inplace = True)
```

# Example of training with just lipids and metabolites

There are samples that are not present in all datasets, we will make different inputs depending on their view-missing patterns.  For example, if the lipids (fist input) are missing, the input will look like:  `[None, torch.tensor(...)]`.

```{python}
# find the missing columns in any datasets and fill them with NA's, the function that builds the batches will look for these all NA columns

all_columns = set(lip.columns).union(metab.columns)

lip_diff = set(all_columns).difference(lip.columns)
lip[list(lip_diff)] = np.nan

metab_diff = set(all_columns).difference(metab.columns)
metab[list(metab_diff)] = np.nan

# align columns
lip = lip[metab.columns]

# transform to N x P tensors
views = [lip, metab]
views = [torch.tensor(el.values, dtype = torch.float32).T for el in views]
```

```{python}
# function that makes dictionaries of inputs of different view-missing patterns
def build_combo_batches(views, y):
    num_view_missing = []

    for view in views:
        na_views = torch.isnan(view).sum(axis = 1) == view.shape[1]
        num_view_missing.append(na_views)

    which_na = torch.stack(num_view_missing, axis = 1)

    input_dict = {}
    gt_dict = {}

    for comb in torch.unique(which_na, dim = 0):
        inds = (which_na == comb.unsqueeze(0)).all(axis = 1)
        input_batch = [views[i][inds] if not comb[i] else None for i in range(len(views))]

        gt_dict[comb] = y[inds]
        input_dict[comb] = input_batch

    return input_dict, gt_dict
```

```{python}
# construct the input

category_map = {
    "Mock": 0,
    "FM": 1,
    "AH1": 2,
    "691": 3
}

# assign classes depending on whether the keys in category map are found in the columns
y_series = pd.Series([category_map[k] for cname in lip.columns for k in category_map.keys() if k in cname])

y = torch.tensor(y_series.values, dtype = torch.int64)

input_dict, gt_dict = build_combo_batches(views, y)

joint_model = make_joint_vae(views, prediction_dim = y_series.nunique(), hidden_sizes = [[64, 64], [32, 32]], z_dim = 16, hidden_dim=32)
optimizer = AdamW(joint_model.parameters())
```

Here we train with the view-missing patterns.  You can also randomly drop a view during training by simply setting it to None in the input list.

```{python}
for epoch in range(1500):
    optimizer.zero_grad()

    for comb, x in input_dict.items():
        y_gt = gt_dict[comb]
        yhat, poe_dist, yhats, dists = joint_model(x)
        # pass alpha and gamma to do focal loss
        loss = joint_model.loss(y_gt, yhat, poe_dist, yhats, dists)

        print(f"loss for {comb}:  {loss.item()}")

        # accumulate the loss from each view-missing pattern
        loss.backward()

    torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(joint_model.parameters(), 2.0)
    optimizer.step()
```

```{python}
# performance on training set
all_logits = []

with torch.inference_mode():
    for k, v in input_dict.items():
        yhat, _, _, _ = joint_model(v)
        all_logits.append(yhat)

all_logits = torch.cat(all_logits)

# get auc score:
auc_score = roc_auc_score(y.numpy(), all_logits.detach().numpy(), multi_class = 'ovr')
print(auc_score)
```
